zh.lifeSummary: 當Grand Prince of Kiev Yurii I. Levovich Rurik於大約1257年4月24日出生在烏克蘭Івано-ФранківськаГалицький район時，他的父親King of Halich Lev I. Danilovich Rurik30歲，母親Boldog Konstancia Árpád-házi20歲。他於1282年與Yaroslavna Princess Of Tver結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1308年3月18日享年50歲。

nameMeanings.name: Rurik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ukrainian and Rusyn (standard transliteration Buryk): nickname derived from buryy ‘dark brown’.

Slovak (mainly Burík and Búrik) and Rusyn (from Slovakia; also Burík): nickname from the archaic Slovak name burík for a (dark) gray horse, or derived directly from an adjective meaning ‘(dark) gray’ (in Slovak) or ‘dark brown’ (in Rusyn; compare 1 above).
Czech (Buřík): variant of Buřič (see Buric 1). Compare Burick .

variants.name: Buric
meanings: Czech (Buřič) and Slovak (Burič): nickname from Czech buřič, Slovak burič ‘rebel’.

Serbian and Croatian (Burić): patronymic from Buro, which may be a short form of the old personal name Burislav (composed of the elements bur, from buriti se ‘to stir up’, + slav, from slava ‘glory’), or a nickname for a big-bellied man derived from the root of burav ‘bellied’.
Croatian (Burić): nickname derived from bura ‘north wind’ (see Bura 2) and given to a child born when this wind was blowing. Compare Burich and Burick .

variants.name: Burick
meanings: Americanized form of Ukrainian, Rusyn, and Slovak Burik or Czech Buřik.
Americanized form of Czech Buřič and Slovak Burič or Serbian and Croatian Burić (see Buric ).

nameMeanings.name: Yurii
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual Russian form of   George  , also used occasionally in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When Grand Prince of Kiev Yurii I. Levovich Rurik was born about 24 April 1257, in Halych, Ivano-Frankivsk, Ukraine, his father, King of Halich Lev I. Danilovich Rurik, was 30 and his mother, Boldog Konstancia Árpád-házi, was 20. He married Yaroslavna Princess Of Tver in 1282. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 18 March 1308, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Grand Prince of Kiev Yurii I. Levovich Rurik est né environ 24 avril 1257, à Halytsʹkyy Rayon, Ivano-Frankivsk, Ukraine, son père, King of Halich Lev I. Danilovich Rurik, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Boldog Konstancia Árpád-házi, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Yaroslavna Princess Of Tver en 1282. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 18 mars 1308, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Grand Prince of Kiev Yurii I. Levovich Rurik um 24. April 1257 in Halytsʹkyy Rayon, Ivano-Frankivsk, Ukraine geboren wurde, war sein Vater King of Halich Lev I. Danilovich Rurik 30 und seine Mutter Boldog Konstancia Árpád-házi 20. Er hat 1282 Yaroslavna Princess Of Tver geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 18. März 1308 im Alter von 50 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Grand Prince of Kiev Yurii I. Levovich Rurik è nato il circa 24 aprile 1257 nel Halytsʹkyy Rayon, Ivano-Frankivsk, Ucraina, il suo padre, King of Halich Lev I. Danilovich Rurik, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Boldog Konstancia Árpád-házi, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Yaroslavna Princess Of Tver nel 1282. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 18 marzo 1308, all'età di 50 anni.
